questions.question: Statement: Descriptive Analytics, is the conventional form of Business Intelligence and data analysis.
Which of the following is not an example of predictive analytics?
State true or False: Data can be numerical and categorical but cannot be continuous or discrete.
Which of the following is not an example of Ratio Data?
Which of the following is a command to have a message appear on the screen?
What is the output from this print() function call: print('$100 $200 $300'.count('$'), '$100 $200 $300'.count('$', 5, 10), '$100 $200 $300'.count('$', 5))?
State True or false: Statement: data can be generated by machines but not by humans.
Which one of the following is not a classification of Data Analytics?
Which of the following is not a method for describing a sample space?
Two events having nonzero probabilities:

options.a: can be both mutually exclusive and independent
options.b: cannot be both mutually exclusive and independent

questions.question: A question paper contains 90 multiple choice questions. There are 4 alternative answers (A, B, C or D) out of which only one is correct. Mr X answers these questions randomly. What is the probability that X gets a score of at least 10 marks?
State true or False: A distribution can either be discrete or continuous, it can't be both at the same time.
The Poisson probability distribution is which of the following type?
Assuming a binomial experiment with p = 0.5 and a sample size of 100. The expected value of this distribution is?
A hypergeometric probability distribution is identical to:

options.d: None of these alternatives is correct

questions.question: The level of significance is the:

options.a: maximum allowable probability of Type II error
options.b: maximum allowable probability of Type I error

questions.question: When the following hypotheses are being tested at a level of significance of alpha: H0: Mu ≥ 500, Ha: Mu < 500. The null hypothesis will be rejected if the p-value is:
A term that means the same as the term 'variable' in an ANOVA procedure is:

An ANOVA procedure is used for four samples, each comprised of 30 observations, were taken from the four populations. The numerator and denominator (respectively) degrees of freedom for the critical value of F are:
Which of the following is not a required assumption for the analysis of variance?

options.a: The random variable of interest for each population has a normal probability distribution
options.b: The variance associated with the random variable must be the same for each population
options.c: At least 2 populations are under consideration

questions.question: In a regression and correlation analysis if r2 = 1, then Sum of square of Error (SSE):

options.a: SSE must also be equal to one
options.b: SSE must be equal to zero
options.c: SSE can be any positive value

questions.question: In a regression analysis if Sum of square of Error (SSE) = 200 and Sum of square of Regression (SSR) = 300, then the coefficient of determination is:
Regression analysis was applied between demand for a product (Y) and the price of the product (X): Y = 120 - 10X. If price is increased by 2 units, then demand is expected to:
Regression analysis was applied between sales (Y in $1,000) and advertising (X in $100): Y = 80 + 6.2X. If advertising is $10,000, then the point estimate for sales (in dollars) is:
In regression analysis if the dependent variable is measured in dollars, the independent variable:

options.b: must be in some units of currency

questions.question: If the coefficient of correlation is 0.90, then the coefficient of determination:

options.c: can be either negative or positive

questions.question: In a regression analysis, the error term is a random variable with a mean or expected value of:
In a regression analysis, the coefficient of determination is 0.4225. The coefficient of correlation in this situation is:
If the coefficient of correlation is 0.8, the percentage of variation in the dependent variable explained by the variation in the independent variable is:
If the coefficient of determination is equal to 1, then the coefficient of correlation:

options.c: can be any value between -1 to +1

questions.question: A simple linear regression equation (y = mx + c) will always pass through the point:
Which of the following methods do we use to best fit the data in Logistic Regression?
Which of the following evaluation metrics can not be applied in case of logistic regression output to compare with target?
Which of the following option is true?

options.a: Linear Regression errors values has to be normally distributed but in case of Logistic Regression it is not the case
options.b: Logistic Regression errors values has to be normally distributed but in case of Linear Regression it is not the case
options.c: Both Linear Regression and Logistic Regression error values have to be normally distributed
options.d: Both Linear Regression and Logistic Regression error values have not to be normally distributed

questions.question: For categorical data with 'n' categories, the number of dummy variables will be:
In binary logistic regression:

options.b: The dependent variable is divided into two equal subcategories
options.c: The dependent variable consists of two categories

questions.question: If the number of False negatives is 5 and number of True Positives is 20, the value of recall will be equal to:
If the precision is 0.6 and the recall value is 0.4, the value of f-measure will be:
Let f(x) denote the logistic function. The range of f(x) for any real value of x is:
An important application of the chi-square distribution is:

options.a: making inferences about a single population variance
options.c: testing for the independence of two variables
options.d: All of these alternatives are correct

questions.question: State True or False: The shape of Chi-square distribution is left-skewed.
Determine the chi-square statistic for the candy flavour data (Apple=180, Lime=250, Cherry=120, Orange=225, Grape=225, Expected=200 each):
The degrees of freedom for a contingency table with 6 rows and 3 columns is:
In K-means clustering, K stands for:
What is the most appropriate no. of clusters for the data points represented by the dendrogram shown?
What is the advantage of Hierarchical clustering over K-means clustering?

options.a: Hierarchical is computationally faster than K-means
options.b: You don't have to assign the number of clusters from the beginning in the case of hierarchical clustering

questions.question: The Euclidean and Manhattan distance between X(3,4) and the origin is respectively:
Which clustering technique requires a merging approach?
Which of the following is required by K-means clustering?

options.c: initial guess as to cluster centroids

questions.question: K-means algorithm, after current iteration, we have 3 clusters A, B, C with centroids (0,1), (2,1), (-1,2). Which cluster will the points (2,3) and (2,0.5) be assigned to in the next iteration?
State True or False: Irrespective of the initial choice of parameters, K-means algorithm will always converge to the same final clusters.
Which one of the following are Decision Tree nodes?
What are the advantages of Classification and Regression Trees (CART)?

options.a: Decision trees require relatively less effort from users for data preparation
options.b: Nonlinear relationships between parameters do not affect tree performance

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of Classification and Regression Trees (CART)?

options.a: Decision trees can be unstable because small variations in the data might result in a completely different tree being generated
options.b: Decision trees require relatively less effort from users for data preparation
options.c: Nonlinear relationships between parameters do not affect tree performance
options.d: Decision trees implicitly perform variable screening or feature selection

questions.question: Suppose, your target variable is whether a passenger will survive or not using Decision Tree. What type of tree do you need to predict the target variable?
What is the maximum depth in a decision tree?

options.a: the length of the longest path from a root to a leaf
options.b: the length of the shortest path from a root to a leaf
options.c: the length of the longest path from a root to a sub-node

questions.question: Suppose in a classification problem, you are using a decision tree, and you use the Gini index as the criterion to select the feature for the root node. The feature with the _____ Gini index will be selected.
In Decision Trees, for predicting a class label, the algorithm starts from which node of the tree?
What is splitting in the decision tree?

options.a: Dividing a node into two or more sub-nodes based on if-else conditions
options.b: Removing a sub-node from the tree
options.c: Balance the dataset prior to fitting
